name: rate-limit
description: v0.5 middleware — fixed-window rate limiter using @blokjs/in-memory-kv. The bucket key is `ctx.state.identity.token` if auth-check ran first, falling back to the X-Forwarded-For header. Each request reads the current bucket counter, increments it, and rejects with 429 + Retry-After when the counter exceeds the per-window limit. Demo defaults: 5 requests per 60-second window. The bucket auto-resets when the window's `expiresAt` timestamp passes — checked on every request via the same expr step, no background reaper needed.

inputs.expression: ((b) => { const now = Date.now(); const limit = 5; const windowMs = 60000; if (!b || typeof b.expiresAt !== 'number' || b.expiresAt <= now) { return { count: 1, expiresAt: now + windowMs, exceeded: false, retryAfterSec: 0 }; } if (b.count + 1 > limit) { return { count: b.count, expiresAt: b.expiresAt, exceeded: true, retryAfterSec: Math.max(1, Math.ceil((b.expiresAt - now) / 1000)) }; } return { count: b.count + 1, expiresAt: b.expiresAt, exceeded: false, retryAfterSec: 0 }; })((ctx.state['load-bucket'] && ctx.state['load-bucket'].value) || null)
